3. but albeit the wise sort of heathens do by light of nature know , that every vicious man be a slave or servant to his own lust or desire : yet the great danger which they apprehend from the servitude , be but fear of satirical censure for preposterous baseness in subject reason , to sensuality . that their own desire , lust , or affection , be maintain and cherish by a foreign enemy , as so many rebel to wage war against their immortal soul , or that their conscience be subdue by lust , shall be everlasting subject to so cruel a tyrant as the devil be , be point wherein the prince of darkness have blind the eye of the wise heathen . and will to God we Christians , to who the lord have reveal thus much , can see or hear so much concern this doctrine , as will make we perfect understand , or lie to heart the inestimable danger wherein we stand , whether in respect of the fast hold which this tyrant by our corrupt nature and custom have get of we , or of the miserable usage which will follow , if he and sin final prevail against we . but this be a common place , for which every man may find a fit text in his own heart , than any other man can choose for he , and matter of more ample and pertinent discourse upon it , than read of many book can suggest unto he . all that i have here to say concern this point be , to request the reader to examine his own heart , and calculate his non-performance of what ( i presume ) he often serious intend , and perhaps have vow . his duty it be to open the wound of his conscience , either to God alone in secret , or to such as God have appoint for the physician of his soul and conscience . my purpose be to prepare the plaster or medicine , and to inform he how to apply it . CHAP. Viii . the son of God be proper a servant to his father , yet not by birth as he be the son of his handmaid , but by voluntary undergo this hard condition for the redemption of man . encage to free we from this miserable servitude unto sin , ( which alone do wound our conscience ) the son of God do free and voluntary take upon he the form of a servant . the part of his peculiar service be in general two : the one , to conquer Satan , who be by right of conquest our lord : the other , to reconcile we to grace and favour with God ; to make we first servant , than son , and last king and priest to he , and our heavenly father . Theise two part of his peculiar service unto his father , ( for unto he alone he be a servant ) exhibit the most admirable pattern of justice , mercy , and love kindness , as well in God the father , as God the son , that the wit of man or angel can contemplate . first it be a pattern of justice , never after to be parallel for God the father to exact satisfaction for our sin , at the hand of his dear and only son . unto this unmeasurable act , or exercise of justice upon the son of righteousness , his mercy towards we miserable sinner be full commensurable . for whatsoever he suffer for our sake , be from his father and his own mercy and love kindness towards we . again , so infinite be the justice of our gracious God , that even while he show his mercy , and love kindness towards we , he do vouchsafe to give ( as we say ) the devil himself his due , and to observe the law of arm or Duell with this prince of rebel , his subject by right of creation , but profess enemy by resolution . albeit this grand rebel after his revolt from God , have conquer man , and make he ( by treachery ) of God servant and son , a mere slave unto himself ; the righteous lord will not deprive this mighty lion , and greedy wolf of his prey , by any other mean , than by right of conquest get over he by man . he do not arm a legion of angel , nor summon the whole host of visible creature against he , nor use his omnipotent and absolute power to destroy or annihilate he , or as then to shut he up in the everlasting prison . the exercise of such power whither immediate by the omnipotent creator himself , or by his creature , have be more than Satan match , upon equal term , or weapon . exercise of strength be not the first way in the wisdom of God to conquer pride , ambition , or vain glory , though these must be quell with the power and strength of the son of God , who it please the father at the first onset to weaken , by lay our first parent infirmity , and their posterity upon he . Theise be the weapon by which he foil the old serpent , and obtain the victory by manage our weakness and infirmity , better than our first parent do those great ability , wherewith their creator have endow they to resist temptation . the weapon which the old serpent use in the conquest of our first parent , and by which he retain their posterity in continual slavery , be their own desire and affection ; these he improve so far that they become unwieldy . and he have get ( as we say ) the better end of the staff , do wrest our will at his pleasure , to do those thing which God forbid we to do , and make we furious executioner of his cunning contrivance against our own soul . the particularity of his sleight or cunning for bring we into thraldom inextricable , ( unless the son of God set we free ) be elsewhere decipher . Theise two be the main general : first , the extension of our natural desire of thing within their bound good and pleasant . second , the improvement of our fear of thing distasteful to nature , as of death , disgrace or torture . now that the son of God may thus beat he at his own weapon , it be necessary that he shall first take upon he the form or essential condition of a servant : for without this , first voluntary undertake by he , the rule of justice can not possible have suffer he , to have suffer so much as he do , for our redemption . wherein then do the state , or condition of a servant , which he take upon he formal consist ? or when do he first become a servant ? from the first moment of his birth or conception ? 2. i can brook their opinion , who think our saviour be by birth a legal servant , as be filius ancillae , the son of a handmaid or bondwoman . this gross heresy have be well refute by some late schoolman , who name i now remember not , nor the name of the author or abettor of this opinion . the mother of the son of God be indeed ancilla , a handmaid , but to he only who service be perfect freedom . So the psalmist in the person of the son of God ( to be manifest in our flesh ) or as his type , direct his prayer , Psal. 116.16 . oh lord true i be thy servant , and the son of thy handmaid . Christ ( as all Christians grant ) be the son of God handmaid , after such a manner , and in such a sense , as never any man beside he be . for he be the promise woman seed , and the son of a woman in such a sort , as he be not the son of any man . again , he be the servant of God , after such a peculiar manner , as neither man or woman have be , or ever shall be . but how do this peculiar service of his fit our servitude unto sin ? even as the medicine do the disease , or as the plaster do the wound for which it be prepare . in the son of God make man there be two distinct will ; the one true divine , the other true humane . to deny this distinction of will in Christ , be to revive the heresy of the monothelite so call , because they hold but one will in Christ ; to wit , the divine . a error into which they haply fall ( as many since their time have do into a worse ) by not distinguish between volunta , and arbitrium . our saviour Christ while he live here on earth , have a reasonable will of the same nature , or quality our will be of ( sin except . ) and by this will he can not but desire his own particular good ; as health , welfare , and other lawful contentment of the humane nature , which be requisite to true joy or happiness . but in as much as the son of God from the begin of man servitude unto Satan become our surety , and to make satisfaction for our sin do in the fullness of time take our nature upon he : he do whole submit his reasonable will , all his affection and desire unto the will of his heavenly father . and in this renounce of the arbitrament of his will , and in the entire submission of it unto the will of his father , do that form of a servant , whereof our apostle speak , formal consist . for unto the essential definition , or constitution of a servant , these two only concur . first , the use of reason . ( for fool , infant , or reasonless creature can be servant ) second , Carentia arbitrii proprii , want of right or arbitrary power to dispose of their bodily action or employment , accord to the this peculiar condition of a servant which the son of God do voluntary take upon he , that main objection which some modern arrian or photinians make against the absolute satisfaction of our lord redeemer , for our sin , may easy be answer , or rather will dissolve it self . God ( say these man ) can not without tyrannical injustice require full satisfaction for the misdemeanour of all wicked and naughty man from one most just , and holy man . to slay the righteous with the wicked , that be far from thou , oh lord , be the say of Abraham to God , Gen. 18.25 . but far sure it be , and always have be , from the judge of all the world , ( who be the eternal live rule of justice it self ) to put the innocent and righteous , to the linger and cruel torture of a ignominious death , for redeem wicked and cruel man from deserve death ; or to purchase not the impunity only , but the advancement of wilful rebel , by the severe punishment of his dear , and only obedient son . the 7. book of the commentary upon the creed . cha . 25. § . 3 , 4 , etc this objection ( as be in the former treatise intimate ) will pierce deep , if we be disarm of those christian principle which these modern heretic have cast aside , to wit , the plurality of person in the Trinity , and the oneness of person in the son of God , Christ Jesus , God and man ; even while he be invest with the form of a servant . we believe , and confess as they do , there be but one god ; and yet in this God , we acknowledge as they do not , unum and alium , one person of the father , another of the son , another of the holy ghost , such a distinction of capacity that the father , not the son exact satisfaction for man violation of the eternal and indispensable rule of equity and justice : that God the son , not God the father , do become man surety , and undertake to make full satisfaction for all his sin . 4. now he that will make satisfaction to another must have somewhat to give of his own , so his own , as it be not the other to who it be give . what then have the son of God to give by way of satisfaction unto God the father , or to the holy ghost , which be so his own , as it be not they ? only that part of our nature which he take from the substance of his mother into the unity of his divine person . in all other part of our nature , over all other part of this universe , God the father , and God the holy ghost have the same interest , or right of dominion with the son . now this part or our nature be thus assume into the unity of the second person : the son of God , and the son of the bless virgin do not differ , as party and party . there be unum and aliud , one nature of the godhead , another of the manhood , non unus , and alius , not one person of the godhead , another of the manhood . the divine nature in the person of the son , be the only party which undertake our redemption : the humane nature assume into the unity of his person , be but his qualification , a appendance , or appurtenance , no true part of his person . and ( as heretofore have be observe ) albeit the flesh of the man Christ Jesus be Caro humana , non divina , the 7. book of commentary upon the creed . Chap. 30. § 10. etc flesh of the same nature and substance with our flesh : yet be his flesh and blood more true the flesh and blood of the son of God , than of the man Christ Jesus : the humane body more true and proper his own , than our body be we . now our flesh , and bodily part be say to be our own , not so much because they be part of our nature , as because they be appurtenance of our person , or because we have a peculiar personal right or power so to dispose of they , as to make they no part of our nature . we account it no unnatural part in wise man to cut off any rot or putrify member , rather than suffer the whole body beside utter to perish . in some certain case , public society , or community of man ( none of which have the like peculiar authority over the mean free private member , as every owner of a body natural have over his tooth , his toe , his finger , or other less principal part necessary for some use only : not for the preservation of the whole ) have by public consent design sometime some principal member of the community , sometime member less principal , not condemn of any crime , as sacrifice for redeem other from present danger , or for secure posterity from servitude , or oppression . and when outrage have be commit by great army , the author or principal incentive of the mutiny be unknown , or not convict by legal proof , the expiation have usual be make by decimation . Every ten man have by wholesome discipline of war be punish accord to the demerit of the crime commit . but albeit every ten man since Adam have be by he and his successor consent devote to death , or linger torture far worse than death : their execution can have make no expiation , no satisfaction unto God for the transgression of the whole community . the attempt of the medicine will have increase the malignity of the universal disease . yea albeit the son of God can have be by man entreat to practice this cure which be use by private wise man for preservation of their natural body , or by great commander for prevent mutiny or loss of army : all this have not be sufficient to have redeem the world , or the whole community of man from utter ruin and destruction ; or ( which be worse than both ) from everlasting servitude unto Satan . Men by art , or rather artist , by the guidance of God providence , have find out remedy against venomous disease by medicinal confection of venomous ingredient . the poisonous bite of the scorpion be usual cure by the oil of scorpion : and of the flesh of some serpent , physician make sovereign antidote for prevent poison , or for cure venomous disease . but the venom which the old serpent have diffuse , not through the vein only , but through the whole nature of man , be not curable by this course of physic . the old serpent be to be destroy , but not to become any ingredient in this catholique medicine , whereby the humane nature be to be cure . that , by the wisdom of God be take out of the nature and substance wound , not from the substance which do wound or sting . but this part of the nature wound , which be to be the medicine for the rest , be first to be perfect cure , and thorough purify by personal unition to the son of God . And be thus purify , and cleanse from all spot of sin , it be disfigure and mangle that the blood of it may be as a balsamum , and quintessence to heal the wound , and sore of our corruption . if it be the will and pleasure of the son of God to submit his most holy body unto the good will , and pleasure of his most holy father ; if with his consent and approbation it be bruise , and mangle : here be no wrong do to any man , but on god part rather a document of his unspeakable love unto mankind : love unexpressible on God the father part that will suffer his only son to take upon he the true form of a servant , and undergo such hard service for we : love unexpressible on God the son behalf that do so willing expose his humane body to pain and torture for our redemption . here be no wrong at all either to the son of God from God the father , or to the humane nature of Christ from the godhead , or divine person of the son ; rather all indignity , and harm which be do unto the man Christ Jesus by Satan , and his instrument do redound unto the son of God . the humane nature be the only subject of the wound and pain . the son of God be the only subject ( if we may so speak ) of the wrong , the only party or person wrong by Satan and his instrument ; but no way wrong by the father , much less by himself , as have free power to put that part of our nature which he assume , unto what service soever his father will require . concern this last qualification of the son of God , i have nothing more to say in this treatise , save only how it be foretell , or foreshadowed . the prediction that the son of God , or the Messiah , shall become a servant , be frequent in the old testament , and will here and there interpose themselves in some ensue discussion of his undertake for dissolve the work of Satan . the next inquiry be , how it be foreshadow , or typical foretell . CHAP. ix . God servant job the most illustrious type of the son of God , as he be invest with the form of a servant . encage the form of a servant which the son of God do take upon he , be foreshadow by all those holy man ( prophet or other ) which be by sacred writer instil the servant of God . a title not usual give to many king or priest , not once ( i take it ) by God himself unto Abraham ; though he be the great of holy man , which be but man ; the father of the faithful , whether king , priest , or prophet : the only prophet , priest , or other , which ( to my remembrance ) be instil the friend of God . Moses , Aaron , and David , be sometime instil the servant of God , by God himself . yet be these three ( respective ) more illustrious type of the son of God , as he be to be make king , priest , and prophet , than of he as he take the form of a servant upon he . of Christ Jesus as he be in a peculiar sort the servant of God , Job the most remarkable pattern of patience ( before this son of God be manifest in the flesh ) be the most exact type or shadow ; not for his qualification only , but in his undertake . job conflict with Satan , and wrestle with temptation be more express record , and more emphatical express , than any man beside , before the only son of God become the son of man , and servant to his heavenly father . Satan by special leave obtain from God ( but so obtain by God as challenger ) do combat or play his prize with this servant of God , at two the most prevalent weapon , which his cunning and long experience upon all advantage , which the weakness of man ( from the fall of Adam ) do afford he , can make choice of . and these two weapon be ; [ hope of good thing , and fear of evil temporal ] which this great usurper do presume be at his disposal , either by right of that conquest which he have get over the first man , or can obtain by god permission to ensnare the first man posterity . the direct and full scope of all our hope be felicity ; and so be misery the period of all our fear . unto felicity three sort of good thing be require , bona animae , bona fortunae , bona corporis . the endowment and contentment of the reasonable soul ; health with ability , and lawful contentment of the body ; competency of mean or worldly substance , which be subservient to both the former endowment and contentment of soul and body . no misery can befall man , but either from the want of some one or more of these three good thing which be require to happiness ( as the philosopher conceive it ) or from their contrary . all the evil which man natural fear , be either evil incident to the body ; as sickness , pain , torment , death , want or loss of good , or worldly substance , loss of good name , disgrace or ignominy , imputation of folly , which be no less grievous to the rational part of man , than pain or grief be to the part sensitive ; more grievous by much to ingenuous man , than loss of good , than want or penury . for as a heathen satirist well observe ; nil habet infoelix paupertas durius in se , quam quòd ridiculos homines facit . the shrewd turn that poverty can do to any mortal creature , be to expose he unto contempt or scorn . by fear of all these three evil Satan drive most man into his snare of servitude ; as many , if not more , as he draw into the same snare by hope of good thing . by every one of these three evil , by the very least of they , if we take they single , he have catch so many as he think sufficient to make up this general induction : [ that none can escape his snare or springe , so he may be permit by God to take his opportunity for set they . ] 2. Job be a man as happy as any man before he have be , accord to that scale of happiness which philosopher can hope for in this life , or can make any probable ground of better hope for the life to come . there be a man ( say the text ) in the land of Vz , who name be Job , and that man be perfect and upright , and one that fear God , and eschew evil . this be a full expression than any philosopher can make of the principal part of happiness , that be of a mind rich endow with all kind of virtue moral ; and more than so , with spiritual grace . and there be bear unto he seven son , and three daughter ; these be more than bona corporis , more than part of his personal constitution , which beside these be exceed good . his substance also be seven thousand sheep , and three thousand camel , and five hundred yoke of ox , and five hundred she ass , and a very great household ( or husbandry , great store no doubt of servant which be part of his worldly substance ) so that this man be the great of all the man of the east . here be a great measure of those thing which philosopher call bona fortunae , good of fortune , or ( as we now say ) goodly mean , fair revenue . Job be a rich man for those time in respect of other , than any man this day live be in respect of our time . yet this goodly cedar in his full height be sound within , and straight without , unshake by any blast of former temptation , until the lord himself appoint he to be a dweller with Satan . the challenge make by Satan be very remarkable . there be a day when the son of God come to present themselves before the lord , and Satan come also among they . and the lord say unto Satan , whence come thou ? then Satan answer the lord and say , from go to and fro in the earth , and from walk up and down in it . and the lord say unto Satan . have thou consider my servant Job , that there be none like he in the earth , a perfect and upright man , one that fear God , and eschew evil ? for . 6 , 7 , 8. how light do we regard it , how perfunctory do we celebrate it ? i doubt not , but we all plain see , that by the come of Christ , God be unite unto man , and man reunite unto God ; the rigor and severity of the law abate , grace give , iniquity forgive , and yet how light do we regard it ? how perfunctory do we celebrate it ? some there be that rather desire to be curious inquisitive into the mystery , than to acknowledge the depth of it with modesty : and do endeavour to apprehend that by natural reason , which far pass the flight of humane understanding . but we ( belove ) that think it to be a more secure way , and of less trouble and perplexity , rather to adore the supernatural excellency of it , than to assay with a Scotish and a sottish subtlety , to dive into it ; first of all , let we think upon his cradle , and then afterward upon his crown : let we take our flight from his humility , that we may at the last so be unto the chief height and sublimity . let we consider his conception , his nativity , his poverty , his infancy , his impotency ; and let we meditate upon that bless , bless time , wherein he take upon he not only the weak nature of man , but also the weak age of man . let we fix our thought upon that thrice happy and auspicious day , a day far excel many century of year in goodness , then it do come short of they in extent . a day , whereon majesty invest it self with despicable humility , whereon the word become flesh , God become man . and yet so , that the dignity and integrity of the deity be nothing impeach , although seem degrade and impair ; neither be there any thing want to complete and absolute humanity which be assume . now if any man be so audicious , as to pry into the secrecy of this mystery , and without wit or fear , to address himself to the discovery of the manner of it , he may perchance quick strike sail , and retire with a o altitudo ! and still be as far from the fruit of his labour as from the end . if we look upon the divinity , God the father be , and have always •eene , and be always say to be . both which property , always to have •eene , and to be , be as just attribute •nto the son . for the deity of the son be not distinguish from the deity of the father . the son be •ee that be beget of the father , •oequall with the father , in respect •f his essence , though not in regard •f his office . now if thou inquire , when Christ be beget , than thou must of necessity devolve thy imagination upon some parcel of time , unto which the deity can no way be •onfined . and the son , be if i may so speak ) parallel to the father , be as well beyond the bound of time , as the father . for how can the son be limit by time , which be , and have be with the father before all time ? beget before all time , as God ; but bear in time as man . as he be God , so he have no cause of his be ; as he be the son , so he may in some sort be say de to have have his origination from the father ; but such a one as be eternal . here may man conceit yield it self to be but shallow ; here against the marble of this difficulty , may the edge of all subtility be rebate : here may the illiterate presume to know as much as the learned . for whatsoever be , and have be before all time , well may it be credit , it can never be comprehend . for that that have be eternal before the existence or essence of man , be no less beyond the reach and capacity of man . for as the imagination and understanding of man , can be ever draw beyond the begin of time , or the extent : so neither without time be ever any man beget , beside Christ which be man and god , and as upon this day be bear man : who if we surmise to have be make , that be blasphemy : if we think that he be not beget , we shall then derogate much from the deity : if we deny his humanity , we then run the hazard of lose our eternal safety , be the fruit and end of his nativity . thus ( to speak in general ) must we acknowledge every thing in God to be far above the strain of reason , but nothing beyond the reach of faith . no less ought we to conceive of God the son in particular . for he be also total God , as he be total man ; and yet not total God because he be also man ; nor total man , because he be also God . o ineffable union ! Surely , this conjunction and combination of the divine and humane nature , proceed only from the immediate and sole act of the deity . and therefore now , oh man , see that thou adore and reverence this mystery , and upon this day think upon thy happy estate and condition , purchase by the obedience and humility of thy saviour : who be bear as upon this day , be notwithstanding beget from everlasting : and be god , for man sake , become man : there be no way to save man but by die for man , and no way to dye for man , but by be man . here may , we behold both his deity and humanity : the one must worthy be esteem , because the other be worthy to be admire . man can not be make God , and therefore God become man and to this end and purpose , that thou , oh man , may acknowledge thy creator power , and embrace thy saviour lowliness ; that so , although his majesty confound thou , yet his humility may comfort thou .
